title: pseudognaphalium affine
intro: Pseudognaphalium affine, also known as Drummond's ragwort or Drummond's everlasting, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium affine include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and even help with menstrual cramps and other female reproductive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
